jokes.text: Sal's enthusiastic but awful Revolutionary War British accent attempts: 'Oi, is that... move that gun over here... and get ready. We're going.' vs 'Gentlemen, strike the flagstaff deep, Sir Knight.'
jokes.setup: Sal wants Larry to judge his British accent for reenactment
jokes.punchline: Both accents are terrible - one generic Cockney, one overly formal nonsense
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Sal's earnest enthusiasm contrasted with terrible execution. Sets up his Revolutionary War obsession that pays off later.

jokes.text: Larry's confessional about being trapped in conversations with Sal: 'The worst mistake I ever made was rolling down that window, in the first place. Just to be nice!'
jokes.setup: Larry explains his predicament with the overly chatty security guard
jokes.punchline: His politeness has trapped him in an endless cycle of unwanted conversations
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David social observation - how politeness becomes a prison. Perfectly captures universal experience with excellent character specificity.

jokes.text: Larry's dark confession: 'Secretly, I wish ill things on him so I don't have to do this anymore. It's a terrible thought, but that's what I think.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about being trapped in unwanted social interactions
jokes.punchline: Larry admits to wishing harm on someone just to avoid small talk
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and honest. Classic Larry - taking a relatable annoyance to its logical but morally questionable extreme. High craft for psychological specificity.

jokes.text: Larry challenging his friend about reading John Adams biography: 'I don't think you read it in the first place.' 'What is this, "Dragnet"?'
jokes.setup: Friend wants his book back and claims to have read it thoroughly
jokes.punchline: Larry's interrogation style compared to police procedural
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's suspicious nature. The Dragnet reference is apt and economical.

jokes.text: Larry's neurotic chef face request: 'if the chef makes a face of any kind, okay, just tell him to forget it and sauté it.'
jokes.setup: Larry wants fish prepared differently but is anxious about chef's reaction
jokes.punchline: Any facial expression is grounds for abandoning the request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry neurosis - overthinking a simple restaurant request. Sets up major callback sequence beautifully.

jokes.text: Larry's baby comment: 'You know, she looks a little Asian.'
jokes.setup: Ken proudly shows baby photos to friends
jokes.punchline: Larry makes inappropriate observation about baby's appearance
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Larry moment. Not the funniest line itself but creates massive discomfort that drives plot. Great callback setup.

jokes.text: Larry doubling down: 'I'm not saying... something's on about it. It's good. It's a good thing.' 'That's a compliment.' 'She's exotic. I wish I looked a little Asian.'
jokes.setup: Ken is offended by the Asian baby comment
jokes.punchline: Larry keeps digging deeper trying to make it a compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of cringe. Larry's inability to let things go creates compound embarrassment. 'I wish I looked a little Asian' is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Larry's non-apology: 'It's my fault for offending you. I apologize. But she does look a little Asian.'
jokes.setup: Ken wants Larry to take back his comment and apologize
jokes.punchline: Larry gives fake apology then immediately repeats the offense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David moment - the form of an apology that completely negates itself. Excellent structure and character consistency.

jokes.text: Mail carrier correction exchange: 'Are you, uh... are you the new mailman?' 'Mailwoman?' 'Mail carrier.'
jokes.setup: Larry tries to use correct terminology for female postal worker
jokes.punchline: The awkward dance of political correctness
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about modern terminology. Competent but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Larry's mother story: 'my mother used to encourage me to work in the post office when I was younger 'cause she didn't think I could amount to anything.'
jokes.setup: Larry making conversation with mail carrier about the postal service
jokes.punchline: His mother's low expectations revealed
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that reveals character history. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Larry's anti-stickler rant: 'Don't be such a stickler. Why are you being such a stickler? Do you think that's a good quality, stickling? Don't stickle. Don't be a stickler.'
jokes.setup: Jean refuses to deliver book without proper postage
jokes.punchline: Larry's repetitive assault on the concept of rule-following
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and word play. Larry's obsessive repetition of 'stickler' variations is both funny and character-specific.

jokes.text: Larry's dating advice: 'if you were going on a computer date, one of those things, and you put down "stickler," nobody would call you.'
jokes.setup: Larry arguing against being a stickler for rules
jokes.punchline: Stickler is an unattractive dating quality
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about dating profiles. Economical and relatable.

jokes.text: Jeff's mysterious kitchen praise: 'You, especially, are going to appreciate it.' 'Why are you especially?' 'I have no fucking idea.'
jokes.setup: Susie says Richard will especially appreciate their new kitchen
jokes.punchline: Jeff admits he has no idea why she said that
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment. Jeff's honest confusion about his wife's random statement is endearing.

jokes.text: Richard's garbage woman story revelation and Larry's disbelief: 'I never even saw a garbage woman in my life. You make up this story to me about fucking a garbage woman?'
jokes.setup: Richard claims to have dated a garbage woman
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous reaction to the implausible story
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurdity. Larry's outrage at Richard's questionable story is both funny and in character.

jokes.text: Larry's 'save the date' rant: 'That's so stupid. I don't save dates. The whole system's screwed up. You don't need to send me something to tell me you're gonna send me something, just send it.'
jokes.setup: Sammi explains the save the date vs invitation system
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical objection to redundant communication
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry social observation. Perfect logic applied to absurd social convention. Highly quotable and universally relatable.

jokes.text: Larry's communication analogy: 'If I'm saying something to you, I don't say to you, "I'm gonna say something to you," and then say it. I just say it.'
jokes.setup: Continuing the save-the-date argument
jokes.punchline: Perfect analogy showing the redundancy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural analysis of social convention. Perfectly economical and logical. One of the episode's best lines.

jokes.text: Multiple 'thank you for your service' repetitions and Larry's silence
jokes.setup: Everyone thanks Victor for his military service
jokes.punchline: Larry conspicuously doesn't participate
jokes.explanation: Good setup for main conflict. Larry's awkward silence amid the chorus of thanks creates tension.

jokes.text: Jeff confronting Larry: 'You didn't thank him for his service, asshole'
jokes.setup: Larry failed to thank Victor
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt accusation and Larry's defensive response about others already thanking him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the thank you for service conflict.

jokes.text: Victor's confrontation: 'You didn't thank him for his service, asshole. They thanked him. Three people thanked him. Why do I have to thank him?'
jokes.setup: Victor calls out Larry for not thanking him
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive logic about redundant thanking
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict. Larry's defensive rationalization is both funny and typically stubborn.

jokes.text: Larry's sarcastic thanks series: 'Thank you for serving the hors d'oeuvres. Thank you for being a very good manager. Thank you for coming over in this brisk weather.'
jokes.setup: Larry is being kicked out for not thanking Victor
jokes.punchline: He sarcastically thanks everyone for mundane things
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and sarcasm. Larry's petty revenge through mock gratitude is perfectly in character and very funny.

jokes.text: Larry's mail caddy idea: 'You could use a caddy on the route. They carry the mail for you... I'll mail caddy for you.'
jokes.setup: Larry compares mail delivery to golf
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous concept of a mail caddy
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist concept. The mail caddy idea is genuinely original and fits Larry's golf-obsessed character.

jokes.text: The movie theater candy mixing concept
jokes.setup: Mail carrier wants to mix sweet and salty movie candy
jokes.punchline: Larry's horror: 'They're in conflict' vs her 'They're in cahoots'
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast in philosophies. 'In cahoots' is a great response to 'in conflict'.

jokes.text: Larry's beckoning argument: 'Just wave. You gotta go like this so I can see you. Just look around. I'm right here. You... I can't see anything from down there.'
jokes.setup: Jean says she shouldn't have to wave in the theater
jokes.punchline: Larry's insistence on proper beckoning protocol
jokes.explanation: Good Larry logic about social expectations. His insistence on proper theater etiquette is both reasonable and ridiculous.

jokes.text: Larry's prepared witticism about 'Shecooktus'
jokes.setup: Larry admits he prepares witticisms for dates
jokes.punchline: Reveals his made-up Jewish holiday 'Shecooktus' was pre-planned
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke about comedy preparation. 'Shecooktus' is absurd and the admission of premeditation is perfect Larry.

jokes.text: Larry's dating performance preparation: 'I gotta psych myself up for dates. It's a big performance. I gotta shower and shave and prepare witticisms'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why daily contact makes dating difficult
jokes.punchline: His clinical description of dating preparation
jokes.explanation: Excellent insight into Larry's dating psychology. The clinical approach to romance is both honest and funny.

jokes.text: Larry's prepared witticism example: 'that whole thing about the made-up Jewish holiday "Shecooktus," that was all... that was prepared beforehand, yeah.'
jokes.setup: Jean asks if he prepared witticisms for their date
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals his fake Jewish holiday joke was pre-planned
jokes.explanation: Great meta-humor about comedy preparation. 'Shecooktus' is a genuinely funny made-up holiday name, and Larry's honesty about preparation is endearing.

jokes.text: Reset button conversation: 'I'd love to hit the reset button' 'No.' 'Why not... wha... no?' 'Why?' 'Yeah, you reset. Let's hit the button.'
jokes.setup: Larry wants to reset their relationship to professional only
jokes.punchline: His persistent button metaphor and her flat refusal
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the reset theme. Larry's obsession with the button metaphor is funny and shows his desire for simple solutions.

jokes.text: Jean's mysterious face reaction when Larry mentions the reset button
jokes.setup: Larry keeps pushing the reset button concept
jokes.punchline: Jean makes an unspecified face that ends their conversation
jokes.explanation: Connects to the chef face investigation theme. The mysterious face creates intrigue but isn't independently funny.

jokes.text: Sal's rabbit ears monologue: 'What ever happened to rabbit ears? I can't tell you how many times my cable has gone down right in the middle of one of my favorite shows.'
jokes.setup: Sal launches into another unwanted conversation topic
jokes.punchline: His nostalgic rambling about outdated TV technology
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency. Sal's boring observations about technology perfectly capture the mundane conversations Larry wants to escape.

jokes.text: Larry's reset attempt with Sal: 'I'd like to hit the reset button on our relationship and... and go back to the way it was before I ever put the window down'
jokes.setup: Larry tries the same reset approach he used with Jean
jokes.punchline: Repeating the exact same strategy with the security guard
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Larry's pattern. The repetition of the reset concept across relationships is both funny and character-revealing.

jokes.text: Chef Curtis's face demonstration: Larry asking him to recreate his reaction to the sole request
jokes.setup: Larry wants to see the actual face the chef made
jokes.punchline: The chef demonstrates his disapproving expression
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff. The absurdity of reconstructing facial expressions for analysis is perfectly Larry-esque.

jokes.text: Larry's horse whinny description: 'You did a semi-horse whinny?' and the chef's confused agreement
jokes.setup: Larry analyzes the chef's facial expression
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous categorization of the face as a 'semi-horse whinny'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist description. 'Semi-horse whinny' is a wonderfully specific and ridiculous way to categorize a facial expression.

jokes.text: The competing face demonstrations: waiter vs chef showing different versions of the same reaction
jokes.setup: Larry gets both parties to demonstrate their version of the face
jokes.punchline: Two completely different interpretations of the same moment
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and visual comedy. The 'clash of faces' perfectly captures how differently people remember the same interaction.

jokes.text: Larry's summary: 'It's a clash of faces.'
jokes.setup: Two different versions of the chef's reaction are demonstrated
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact description of the discrepancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic summation. 'Clash of faces' is brilliantly economical and captures the absurdity while sounding like serious analysis.

jokes.text: Mr. Takahashi's formal accusation: 'It has been brought to my attention that you have disgraced Ken Carmen and his wife, Shelly.'
jokes.setup: Larry is summoned to the club manager's office
jokes.punchline: Formal charges for calling a baby Asian-looking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of consequences. The formal language for such a minor offense is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Soon argument: 'Expect a letter soon.' 'Soon? A day? Two days? Four days?' 'Everybody know what "soon" is.' 'Soon is not soon.'
jokes.setup: Mr. Takahashi says Larry will get a letter 'soon'
jokes.punchline: Larry's obsessive need to define 'soon' precisely
jokes.explanation: Great Larry obsession with precision. The circular argument about 'soon' captures his need to control and define everything.

jokes.text: Jeff's Burger King analogy: 'Had the same situation with a girl at Burger King. Haven't had a Whopper in fucking three years.'
jokes.setup: Larry complains about not getting mail due to dating the mail carrier
jokes.punchline: Jeff's parallel situation with fast food worker
jokes.explanation: Excellent parallel situation. Jeff's Whopper deprivation perfectly mirrors Larry's mail situation and shows the universal nature of the problem.

jokes.text: Jeff's confused sexual questioning: 'You didn't tap that ass?' 'You didn't hit it?' 'And you going through the same shit if you had hit it?'
jokes.setup: Jeff assumes Larry slept with Jean
jokes.punchline: His disbelief that Larry faces consequences without getting sex
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jeff. His incredulity at Larry suffering dating consequences without sexual benefits is crude but funny.

jokes.text: Sal's revenge reveal: Larry discovers he's 'at the very top of the do not admit list'
jokes.setup: Larry tries to enter the club to get his letter
jokes.punchline: His reset attempt backfired spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and ironic reversal. Larry's attempt to simplify relationships has created maximum complexity.

jokes.text: Larry's angry rant: 'I was the only one in this club who ever spoke to you... So unbelievably boring... about your fucking cat.'
jokes.setup: Larry is banned from the club by the person he was nice to
jokes.punchline: His explosive honesty about Sal's boring conversations
jokes.explanation: Perfect character explosion. Larry's frustration boiling over into brutal honesty is both cathartic and destructive.

jokes.text: Larry's rant about being the only one who talked to Sal
jokes.setup: Larry is banned from the club by Sal
jokes.punchline: His angry revelation that he was the only member who ever spoke to Sal
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic climax - Larry's kindness is what gets him punished. The complaint about 'bullshit and drivel' is cathartic.

jokes.text: Larry's final insult: 'I was suffocating from the bullshit and drivel that was coming out of your mouth'
jokes.setup: Larry's anger at being banned continues
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about years of forced politeness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's honest rage. The 'suffocating' metaphor effectively captures years of social politeness.

jokes.text: Larry's premature Revolutionary War death: 'What are you... what are you doing? You got three hours to go, here. Are you crazy? Get up. This is nuts.'
jokes.setup: Larry is 'killed' in the first battle of the reenactment
jokes.punchline: He refuses to stay dead for the remaining three hours
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry reaction to arbitrary rules. His refusal to play dead for hours is both logical and completely ruins the event.

jokes.text: Larry 'dying' immediately and refusing to get up for three hours
jokes.setup: Revolutionary War battle begins with gunfire
jokes.punchline: Larry falls down 'dead' immediately and won't get up despite having 3 hours left
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry commitment to avoiding participation while technically following rules. The three-hour detail is key.

jokes.text: Real cannon fire chaos: 'There's a crater in the ground.' 'This is real! This is real!' 'It's live rounds, Larry!'
jokes.setup: What seemed like harmless reenactment
jokes.punchline: Sal is actually shooting at them with real ammunition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from pretend war to real danger. The shift from historical reenactment to actual combat is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Larry's desperate apology: 'Hello, Sal! I'm sorry!' 'Hello! How's your cat?'
jokes.setup: Larry is under real cannon fire from Sal
jokes.punchline: He desperately tries to reconnect about the boring topics he hated
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal. Larry desperately wanting to discuss the exact topics he previously found insufferable is brilliantly constructed.

jokes.text: Mr. Takahashi affair discovery: Larry catching him with Ken's wife in the car
jokes.setup: Larry approaches Takahashi's car to ask about the letter
jokes.punchline: Discovers the club manager having an affair
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot revelation that recontextualizes the entire episode. The discovery gives Larry leverage and validates his original observation.

jokes.text: Larry's vindication: 'I knew that baby looked a little Asian.'
jokes.setup: Larry discovers the affair explaining the baby's appearance
jokes.punchline: His satisfaction at being proven right
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and vindication. The line perfectly ties together the episode's central conflict with maximum satisfaction.

jokes.text: Victor's mistaken valet attack: 'Redcoats, man!' 'Don't tread on me, motherfuckers!' while attacking innocent golf course valets
jokes.setup: Victor is still in Revolutionary War mindset after the battle
jokes.punchline: He attacks modern golf course employees thinking they're British soldiers
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation. Victor's PTSD-like confusion between historical reenactment and reality is both funny and slightly disturbing.

jokes.text: Jean's final mail delivery: 'Welcome home, soldier. Thank you for your service.'
jokes.setup: Larry returns from the disastrous Revolutionary War reenactment
jokes.punchline: Jean finally gives him his mail and thanks him for his 'service'
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback. Jean's mock gratitude for Larry's 'military service' beautifully ties together multiple episode themes.
